inputs.question: What is the base class for all entities in Unity scenes?
Which component determines the position, rotation, and scale of a GameObject?
In a C# script, what method is called exactly once before the first frame update?
What method is called immediately when a script instance is loaded, before the Start method?
How do you print a message to the Unity Console from a script?
What component do you need to add to a GameObject to make it emit light?
What component is required in a scene for the player to hear audio?
What component is used to play back sound effects and music in a scene?
What system is used to bake navigation data so AI characters can move around obstacles?
What is the built-in 3D physics engine used by Unity?
What is the built-in 2D physics engine used by Unity?
What component applies a physical boundary to a GameObject to allow for collisions?
How do you access the Transform component of the GameObject your script is attached to?
What type of asset is used to define the visual appearance and surface properties of a 3D model?
What UI component automatically arranges its child elements into a list or grid?

outputs.expected: Layout Group (Vertical, Horizontal, or Grid Layout Group)

inputs.question: Which Unity class is primarily used to read input from the keyboard, mouse, or controller?
Which window in the Unity Editor displays a list of all GameObjects currently in the active scene?
Which window in the Unity Editor allows you to view and edit the properties and components of a selected GameObject?
What is a 'Tag' used for in Unity?

outputs.expected: Tags are reference strings assigned to GameObjects to help identify them or group them together.

inputs.question: What is the purpose of Time.deltaTime?

outputs.expected: It represents the time elapsed since the last frame, used to make movement and changes frame-rate independent.
